description: An activity, process, workshop, or garden is 'poru' when it actually yields useful or intended results. 'muroa' (fertile) tells whether conditions can sustain growth; 'poruli' (efficient) compares result with the resources used, and 'thirulo' (output) is the result itself. A productive period describes what happened during that period, not the worth of the person who worked through it.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release them into stressed true-mid rounded back 'o'. Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip for 'r', then raise the back of the tongue into high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: The compact release of 'po' gives way to the brief movement of 'r' and closes on rounded 'u'. Its two firm syllables bring to mind a task that reaches its result without fuss.
usage_notes: Noun use supplies productivity. The adjective sits most naturally with an activity, period, process, place, or system.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A workshop may be productive because it repairs what the community needs, even when it produces no new goods. The useful result is the point.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Productive seasons alternate with rest, maintenance, and fallow time; a year's yield depends on all of them.

semantic_domains.activity: work or a process that reaches useful or intended results
semantic_domains.creation: a workshop, garden, or system that yields an actual result
